message.content: Find and read SpeedometerView.swift in /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode. Also read any closely related files (ViewModel, supporting views) referenced inside it. Return full file contents. Search breadth: medium.

attachment.content:
- make: Run the project Makefile (regenerates Xcode project via xcodegen and opens it).
- pr: Wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
- post-merge: Sync main after a PR has been merged (git checkout main + pull --ff-only + make).
- pdf-viewer:open: Open a PDF in the interactive viewer
- pdf-viewer:fill-form: Fill PDF form fields interactively with live visual feedback
- pdf-viewer:sign: Place a signature or initials image on a PDF
- pdf-viewer:annotate: Collaboratively annotate a PDF — propose markup, review together, iterate
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:extract-design
- pdf-viewer:view-pdf: Interactive PDF viewer. Use when the user wants to open, show, or view a PDF and collaborate on it visually — annotate, highlight, stamp, fill form fields, place signature/initials, or review markup together. Not for summarization or text extraction (use native Read instead).
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text: I'll help you find and read SpeedometerView.swift along with related files. Let me start by locating the file and exploring its dependencies.
Now let me read the main SpeedometerView.swift file and search for related files in the same directory:

content.content:
1	//
2	//  SpeedometerView.swift
3	//  Radiacode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 05.03.2024.
6	//
7	
8	import SwiftUI
9	import Combine
10	
11	struct SpeedometerView: View {
12	    
13	    @ObservedObject var viewModel: SpeedometerViewModel
14	
15	    let radiationType: RadiationType.Level
16	    let alarmLevels: Alarm.Level?
17	    
18	    var body: some View {
19	        Gauge(value: viewModel.currentValue, in: viewModel.range) {
20	        } currentValueLabel: {
21	            RadiationTypeLabelView(value: viewModel.currentValue, radiationType: .radiationLevel(radiationType))
22	                .label { value, unit in
23	                    VStack(alignment: .center, spacing: 0) {
24	                        Text(value)
25	                            .font(.system(size: SearchFontSize.value, weight: .bold, design: .rounded))
26	                        Text(unit)
27	                            .font(.system(size: SearchFontSize.unit))
28	                    }
29	                    .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
30	                    .foregroundStyle(.textPrimary)
31	                }
32	        }
33	        .gaugeStyle(
34	            SpeedometerGaugeStyle(
35	                range: viewModel.range,
36	                radiationType: radiationType,
37	                alarmLevel: alarmLevels?.level1,
38	                criticalLevel: alarmLevels?.level2,
39	                mainColor: mainColor
40	            )
41	        )
42	    }
43	    
44	    private var mainColor: Color {
45	        guard let level = alarmLevels?.checkValue(viewModel.currentValue),
46	              level != .normal else {
47	            return radiationType.accentColor
48	        }
49	        return level.color
50	    }
51	}
52	
53	private struct SpeedometerGaugeStyle: GaugeStyle {
54	    
55	    private enum Consts {
56	        static let angleCoefficient: CGFloat = 0.7505
57	        static let angleDegrees = angleCoefficient * 360
58	        static let mainLineWidth: CGFloat = {
59	            #if os(watchOS)
60	                8
61	            #else
62	                12
63	            #endif
64	        }()
65	        static let startAngle = angleDegrees / 2 * -1
66	    }
67	    
68	    let range: ClosedRange<Float>
69	    let radiationType: RadiationType.Level
70	    
71	    let alarmLevel: Decimal?
72	    let criticalLevel: Decimal?
73	    
74	    let mainColor: Color
75	    
76	    @State private var previousValue: Double = 0.0
77	    
78	    func makeBody(configuration: Configuration) -> some View {
79	        GeometryReader { fullGeometry in
80	            ZStack {
81	                ZStack(alignment: .center) {
82	                    /// White line
83	                    Circle()
84	                        .trim(from: 0, to: Consts.angleCoefficient * configuration.value)
85	                        .stroke(AngularGradient(
86	                            gradient: Gradient(colors: [.clear, .white]),
87	                            center: .center,
88	                            startAngle: .degrees(0),
89	                            endAngle: .degrees(Consts.angleDegrees * configuration.value)), lineWidth: 4)
90	                        .rotationEffect(.degrees(135))
91	                        .padding(-Consts.mainLineWidth * 1.2)
92	                        .animation(previousValue > configuration.value ?
93	                            .easeIn(duration: 2) : .easeIn(duration: 0.5), value: configuration.value)
94	                    
95	                    /// Background
96	                    Circle()
97	                        .trim(from: 0, to: Consts.angleCoefficient)
98	                        .stroke(.dark04, lineWidth: Consts.mainLineWidth)
99	                        .rotationEffect(.degrees(135))
100	                        .animation(.easeIn(duration: 0.5), value: configuration.value)
101	
102	                    /// Dashes
103	                    GaugeView(
104	                        maxValue: range.upperBound,
105	                        numberOfElements: 7,
106	                        numberOfNeedles: 10,
107	                        isDecimal: radiationType == .doseRate,
108	                        coveredRadius: Consts.angleDegrees
109	                    )
110	                    .padding(Consts.mainLineWidth / 2)
111	                    
112	                    if let criticalLevel = criticalLevel?.floatValue, criticalLevel < range.upperBound {
113	                        VStack {
114	                            Rectangle()
115	                                .fill(LinearGradient(
116	                                    gradient: Gradient(colors: [.black, .accentRed.opacity(0.5), .accentRed]),
117	                                    startPoint: .bottom,
118	                                    endPoint: .top
119	                                ))
120	                                .frame(width: 4, height: Consts.mainLineWidth * 2.7)
121	                                .padding(.top, -Consts.mainLineWidth / 2.3)
122	                            Spacer()
123	                        }
124	                        .rotationEffect(.degrees(Consts.startAngle + CGFloat(criticalLevel / range.upperBound) * Consts.angleDegrees))
125	                    }
126	                    
127	                    if let alarmLevel = alarmLevel?.floatValue, alarmLevel < range.upperBound {
128	                        VStack {
129	                            Rectangle()
130	                                .fill(LinearGradient(
131	                                    gradient: Gradient(colors: [.black, .accentYellow.opacity(0.5), .accentYellow]),
132	                                    startPoint: .bottom,
133	                                    endPoint: .top
134	                                ))
135	                                .frame(width: 4, height: Consts.mainLineWidth * 2.7)
136	                                .padding(.top, -Consts.mainLineWidth / 2.3)
137	                            Spacer()
138	                        }
139	                        .rotationEffect(.degrees(Consts.startAngle + CGFloat(alarmLevel / range.upperBound) * Consts.angleDegrees))
140	                    }
141	                                        
142	                    /// Selected shadow
143	                    let width = (fullGeometry.size.width - Consts.mainLineWidth) / 2
144	                    Circle()
145	                        .trim(from: 0, to: Consts.angleCoefficient * configuration.value)
146	                        .stroke(gradient(for: mainColor, width: width), lineWidth: width)
147	                        .rotationEffect(.degrees(135))
148	                        .padding(width / 2 + Consts.mainLineWidth / 2)
149	                        .animation(.easeIn(duration: 0.5), value: configuration.value)
150	                        .overlay {
151	                            configuration.currentValueLabel
152	                        }
153	
154	                    /// Selected Line
155	                    Circle()
156	                        .trim(from: 0, to: Consts.angleCoefficient * configuration.value)
157	                        .stroke(mainColor, lineWidth: Consts.mainLineWidth)
158	                        .rotationEffect(.degrees(135))
159	                        .animation(.easeIn(duration: 0.5), value: configuration.value)
160	                }
161	            }
162	        }
163	        .aspectRatio(contentMode: .fit)
164	        .padding(.horizontal, Consts.mainLineWidth * 1.6)
165	        .padding(.top, Consts.mainLineWidth * 1.6)
166	        .padding(.bottom, -30)
167	        .onChange(of: configuration.value) { oldValue, newValue in
168	            previousValue = newValue
169	        }
170	    }
171	    
172	    private func gradient(for color: Color, width: CGFloat) -> RadialGradient {
173	        RadialGradient(gradient: Gradient(colors: [
174	            .clear,
175	            .clear,
176	            color.opacity(0.4),
177	        ]), center: .center, startRadius: 0, endRadius: width)
178	    }
179	}
180	
181	private struct GaugeView: View {
182	
183	    private let numberOfElements: Int
184	    private let numberOfNeedles: Int
185	    private let isDecimal: Bool
186	
187	    // Calculated
188	    private let startAngle: Double
189	    private let stepper: Double
190	    private let needleStep: Double
191	
192	    private let mainValues: [Double]
193	
194	    init(maxValue: Float, numberOfElements: Int, numberOfNeedles: Int, isDecimal: Bool, coveredRadius: Double) {
195	        self.numberOfElements = numberOfElements
196	        self.numberOfNeedles = numberOfNeedles
197	        self.isDecimal = isDecimal
198	        
199	        let numberOfElementsIndex = Double(numberOfElements - 1)
200	        
201	        self.startAngle = coveredRadius / 2 * -1
202	        self.stepper = coveredRadius / numberOfElementsIndex
203	        self.needleStep = stepper / Double(numberOfElements)
204	       
205	        let step = Double(maxValue) / numberOfElementsIndex
206	        self.mainValues = (0..<numberOfElements).map { Double($0) * step }
207	    }
208	        
209	    var body: some View {
210	        ZStack {
211	            ForEach(mainValues.indices, id: \.self) { index in
212	                let element = mainValues[index]
213	                let rotation = Angle.degrees(startAngle + stepper * Double(index))
214	                if index < numberOfElements - 1 {
215	                    ForEach(1..<numberOfNeedles, id: \.self) { needleIndex in
216	                        VStack {
217	                            Rectangle()
218	                                .fill(.dark04)
219	                                .frame(width: 1, height: 8)
220	                            Spacer()
221	                        }
222	                        .rotationEffect(.degrees(rotation.degrees + needleStep * Double(needleIndex)))
223	                    }
224	                }
225	                VStack {
226	                    Rectangle()
227	                        .fill(.dark04)
228	                        .frame(width: 1, height: 15)
229	                    Text(!isDecimal ? "\(Int(element))" : String(format: "%.1f", element))
230	                        .foregroundStyle(.white)
231	                        .font(.system(size: SearchFontSize.tick, weight: .medium))
232	                        .rotationEffect(-rotation)
233	                    Spacer()
234	                }
235	                .rotationEffect(rotation)
236	            }
237	        }
238	    }
239	}
240	
241	enum SearchFontSize {
242	#if os(watchOS)
243	    private static let isSmallWatch: Bool = {
244	        let width = WKInterfaceDevice.current().screenBounds.width
245	        return width < 170
246	    }()
247	    static let value: CGFloat = isSmallWatch ? 30 : 32
248	    static let unit: CGFloat = 14
249	    static let tick: CGFloat = isSmallWatch ? 10 : 14
250	#else
251	    static let value: CGFloat = 52
252	    static let unit: CGFloat = 28
253	    static let tick: CGFloat = 16
254	#endif
255	}
256	
257	#Preview {
258	    let viewModel = SpeedometerViewModel(
259	        initialMaxValue: 300,
260	        averagingRawDataPublished: PassthroughSubject<Float, Never>().eraseToAnyPublisher(),
261	        queueLabel: "13"
262	    )
263	    viewModel.currentValue = 100
264	    return VStack {
265	        SpeedometerView(
266	            viewModel: viewModel,
267	            radiationType: .countRate,
268	            alarmLevels: .init(radiationType: .radiationLevel(.countRate), level1: 20, level2: 25)
269	        )
270	    }
271	}
272

content.text: Now let me read the ViewModel and search for related types:
Now let me read the supporting model files:

content.content:
1	//
2	//  Alarm.swift
3	//  RadiaCode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 04.09.2025.
6	//
7	
8	import Foundation
9	
10	/// Пороговые значения тревог
11	@Observable
12	final class Alarm: Equatable, Codable {
13	    
14	    enum Consts {
15	        static let initialMaxCountRate: Float = 30.0
16	        static let initialMaxDoseRate: Float = 0.7
17	    }
18	    
19	    /// Уровни тревог
20	    struct Level: Equatable, Codable {
21	        
22	        let radiationType: RadiationType
23	        /// Первый порог
24	        var level1: Decimal?
25	        /// Второй порог
26	        var level2: Decimal?
27	        /// Зашкал
28	        var offScale: Decimal?
29	        
30	        /// Проверка значения на Алерт
31	        /// - Parameter value: значение
32	        /// - Returns: алерт
33	        func checkValue(_ value: Float) -> SignalizationAlarmLevel {
34	            if let offScale = offScale?.floatValue, value >= offScale {
35	                return .offScale
36	            }
37	            if let level2 = level2?.floatValue, value >= level2 {
38	                return .level2
39	            }
40	            if let level1 = level1?.floatValue, value >= level1 {
41	                return .level1
42	            }
43	            return .normal
44	        }
45	        
46	        var isEmpty: Bool {
47	            level1 == nil && level2 == nil
48	        }
49	    }
50	    
51	    /// Пороги по скорости счета [имп. за 10 с]
52	    var countRate: Level
53	    /// Пороги по мощности дозы
54	    var doseRate: Level
55	    /// Пороги по дозе
56	    var dose: Level
57	    
58	    var isEmpty: Bool {
59	        countRate.isEmpty && doseRate.isEmpty && dose.isEmpty
60	    }
61	    
62	    init(
63	        countRate: Level = Level(radiationType: .radiationLevel(.countRate)),
64	        doseRate: Level = Level(radiationType: .radiationLevel(.doseRate)),
65	        dose: Level = Level(radiationType: .dose)
66	    ) {
67	        self.countRate = countRate
68	        self.doseRate = doseRate
69	        self.dose = dose
70	    }
71	    
72	    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
73	        case countRate
74	        case doseRate
75	        case dose
76	    }
77	
78	    required init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
79	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
80	        self.countRate = try container.decode(Level.self, forKey: .countRate)
81	        self.doseRate = try container.decode(Level.self, forKey: .doseRate)
82	        self.dose = try container.decode(Level.self, forKey: .dose)
83	    }
84	
85	    func encode(to encoder: Encoder) throws {
86	        var container = encoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
87	        try container.encode(countRate, forKey: .countRate)
88	        try container.encode(doseRate, forKey: .doseRate)
89	        try container.encode(dose, forKey: .dose)
90	    }
91	    
92	    static func == (lhs: Alarm, rhs: Alarm) -> Bool {
93	        lhs.countRate == rhs.countRate &&
94	        lhs.doseRate == rhs.doseRate &&
95	        lhs.dose == rhs.dose
96	    }
97	}
98

1	//
2	//  RadiationTypeLabelView.swift
3	//  RadiaCode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 25.05.2026.
6	//
7	
8	import SwiftUI
9	
10	struct RadiationTypeLabelView: View {
11	    let value: Float?
12	    let radiationType: RadiationType
13	
14	    var body: some View {
15	        label { valueString, unitString in
16	            RadiationLabelDefaultLayout(valueString: valueString, unitString: unitString)
17	        }
18	    }
19	
20	    @ViewBuilder
21	    func label<Content: View>(
22	        @ViewBuilder _ build: @escaping (String, String) -> Content
23	    ) -> some View {
24	        switch radiationType {
25	        case .radiationLevel(.countRate):
26	            CountRateLabelView(value: value)
27	                .label(build)
28	        case .radiationLevel(.doseRate):
29	            DoseRateLabelView(value: value)
30	                .label(build)
31	        case .dose:
32	            DoseLabelView(value: value)
33	                .label(build)
34	        }
35	    }
36	}
37

1	//
2	//  CountRateLabelView.swift
3	//  RadiaCode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 22.05.2026.
6	//
7	
8	import SwiftUI
9	
10	struct CountRateLabelView: View {
11	    let value: Float?
12	    
13	    var body: some View {
14	        label { value, unit in
15	            RadiationLabelDefaultLayout(valueString: value, unitString: unit)
16	        }
17	    }
18	    
19	    func label<Content: View>(
20	        @ViewBuilder _ build: @escaping (_ value: String, _ unit: String) -> Content
21	    ) -> some View {
22	        _CountRateLabelContent(value: value, build: build)
23	    }
24	}
25	
26	private struct _CountRateLabelContent<Content: View>: View {
27	    
28	    @AppStorage("countRateUnit") private var unit: Settings.CountRateMeasurementUnit = .cps
29	    
30	    let value: Float?
31	    let build: (String, String) -> Content
32	
33	    var body: some View {
34	        let formatted = CountRateFormatted(rawValue: value, unit: unit)
35	        build(formatted.value?.formattedValue ?? "--", String(localized: formatted.unit))
36	    }
37	}

7	
8	import Foundation
9	import SwiftUI
10	
11	enum SignalizationAlarmLevel: Codable, Comparable {
12	    case normal
13	    case level1
14	    case level2
15	    case offScale
16	    
17	    var color: Color {
18	        switch self {
19	        case .normal:

37	            .accentRed

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report with all the file contents.

## Summary

I've located and read SpeedometerView.swift and all closely related files. Here's the complete file contents:

### Primary File

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Presentation/Containers/Search/Speedometer/SpeedometerView.swift`**

This is a SwiftUI gauge component that displays radiation measurements (count rate or dose rate) with an animated semicircular speedometer-style gauge. Key features include:
- Custom gauge style with animated needle showing current value
- Alarm level indicators (yellow for level1, red for level2)
- Support for both watchOS and iOS with different font sizing
- Dashes/ticks with numerical labels around the gauge
- Color-coded display based on alarm state

---

### Related ViewModel

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Presentation/Containers/Search/Speedometer/SpeedometerViewModel.swift`**

This observable object manages the speedometer's data state:
- Receives raw data via a Combine publisher
- Dynamically scales the gauge range (doubles max when value exceeds range)
- Auto-scales down the range if values stay below half the max for 30 seconds
- Publishes `currentValue` and `range` for the view to display

---

### Supporting Models

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Model/RadiationType.swift`**

Enum defining radiation measurement types with their properties:
- `Level`: countRate (cps units, green accent) or doseRate (μSv/h units, blue accent)
- `case dose`: for accumulated dose measurements
- Includes unit conversion logic and default max values

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Model/Alarm.swift`**

Observable alarm threshold configuration:
- `Level` struct with level1, level2, and offScale thresholds
- `checkValue()` method to determine which alarm state a measurement is in
- Thresholds for countRate, doseRate, and dose separately

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Model/SignalizationAlarmLevel.swift`**

Enum for alarm states with associated colors:
- normal → textPrimary
- level1 → accentYellow
- level2 → accentRed
- offScale → accentPink

### Supporting Views

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Model/RadiationLabel/RadiationTypeLabelView.swift`**

Switches between different label view implementations based on radiation type:
- CountRateLabelView
- DoseRateLabelView
- DoseLabelView

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Model/RadiationLabel/CountRateLabelView.swift`**

Displays count rate (impulses per second) with formatting logic controlled by user settings.

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Model/RadiationLabel/DoseLabelView.swift`**

Displays dose or dose rate with dynamic unit conversion (μSv, mSv, or Sv depending on magnitude).

### Design System

**File: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-ios/RadiaCode/Presentation/Design System/Constants/RadColors+Color.swift`**

Color palette extension providing named colors used throughout the app:
- Accent colors: accentBlue, accentRed, accentGreen, accentYellow, etc.
- Monochrome: dark04, dark05, dark06, light02-06, black00, white00
- Semantic colors: fillPrimary, textPrimary, separatorFill, glass effects
